title: China's GDP growth is the pillar for a sustained dry bulk capesize prosperity?
section_path: China's GDP growth is the pillar for a sustained dry bulk capesize prosperity?
section_path_text: China's GDP growth is the pillar for a sustained dry bulk capesize prosperity?
summary:
Main
by Maria Bertzeletou
China's economy grew at the slowest pace in more than four decades last year despite a rebound after the country's coronavirus outbreak. The 2.3% expansion is the lowest figure since the Chinese economy embarked on major reforms in the 1970s. The National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) said 2020 was a "grave and complex environment both at home and abroad" with the pandemic having a "huge impact."
The figure was a marked slowdown from 2019 growth of 6.1% - itself already the lowest in decades - with the country hit by weak domestic demand and trade tensions.
